headline: DEVELOPING: IRGC claims it sank US Navy ships and killed Americans — Pentagon denies; viral video debunked as video game footage (Arma 3)
summary: IRGC claims it struck and sank US Navy vessels including the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier — all denied by CENTCOM. A viral video claiming to show the Lincoln on fire was traced to the Arma 3 video game. In reality, the US has destroyed 90+ Iranian vessels including sinking the IRIS Dena frigate by submarine — the first US sub kill since WWII.
tickerText: DEVELOPING: IRGC claims it struck USS Abraham Lincoln carrier with ballistic missiles, says US ships sunk — CENTCOM categorically denies; viral 'burning carrier' video debunked as Arma 3 game footage; IRGC filmed civilian tanker Safesea Vishnu claiming it was US Navy ship; In reality US has destroyed 90+ Iranian vessels; IRIS Dena frigate torpedoed by USS Charlotte — first US submarine kill since WWII; IRGC Navy commander Tangsiri killed March 26

sections.title: IRGC Claim: USS Abraham Lincoln Struck by Missiles

items.label: March 1 — IRGC claims 4 ballistic missiles hit USS Abraham Lincoln
items.detail: IRGC claimed it targeted the USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72) with four ballistic missiles, saying strikes against US forces had 'entered a new phase.' CENTCOM rejected the claim, stating the Lincoln 'was not hit' and incoming missiles 'didn't even come close.'

items.label: March 5 — IRGC naval drones 'struck Lincoln in Sea of Oman'
items.detail: IRGC naval drone units claimed they struck the Lincoln in the Sea of Oman, forcing it and escort ships to retreat over 1,000 km. CENTCOM confirmed the Lincoln continued normal flight operations throughout.

items.label: March 13 — IRGC repeats claim Lincoln 'withdrawing toward US'
items.detail: IRGC repeated the claim, saying the Lincoln sustained significant damage and was 'withdrawing toward the United States.' CENTCOM explicitly called out Iran's 'false messaging machine' for continuing to falsely claim it had sunk a US vessel.

sections.title: Fake Evidence — Video Game Footage & Misidentified Ships

items.label: Viral 'burning carrier' video is Arma 3 video game footage
items.detail: A viral video purporting to show the USS Abraham Lincoln on fire was traced back to June 23, 2025 and identified as footage from the Arma 3 video game — not real. The video received millions of views before being debunked by fact-checkers.

items.label: IRGC filmed civilian tanker, claimed it was US Navy ship
items.detail: IRGC personnel filmed from a nearby vessel claiming they had 'destroyed a US ship in the Persian Gulf.' The vessel was actually the Safesea Vishnu, a civilian oil tanker — not a US Navy ship.

items.label: 18 false claims identified by NewsGuard
items.detail: NewsGuard identified 18 war-related false claims by Iran (vs. 5 in two weeks prior to the war). AI-doctored images and videos used extensively — e.g., fake Burj Khalifa fire video got 100M+ views. Iran's domestic internet at ~4%, controlling the narrative internally.

sections.title: REALITY — US Has Destroyed 90+ Iranian Naval Vessels

items.label: CENTCOM confirms 90+ Iranian vessels destroyed or damaged
items.detail: The US Navy has destroyed or damaged over 90 Iranian naval vessels since the start of the conflict. Admiral Cooper described the IRGC Navy as on 'irreversible decline.'

items.label: IRIS Dena frigate torpedoed by USS Charlotte — 104 killed
items.detail: The IRIS Dena (frigate) was torpedoed and sunk by USS Charlotte (SSN-766) off Sri Lanka — 104 Iranian sailors killed. This is the first US submarine sinking of an enemy warship since WWII.

items.label: Trump: US destroyed 9 Iranian warships at Hormuz
items.detail: President Trump confirmed early in the conflict that the US sank 9 Iranian warships near the Strait of Hormuz. Additional vessels destroyed include the Shahid Bagheri (drone/helicopter carrier), IRIS Makran (forward base ship), and a Jamaran-class corvette at Chah Bahar.

items.label: IRGC Navy commander Tangsiri killed — March 26
items.detail: IRGC Navy commander Alireza Tangsiri was killed March 26 in a targeted strike, further decimating Iran's naval command structure.

sections.title: IRGC Claims: Americans Killed in Base Attacks

items.label: IRGC claim: 650 US troops killed/wounded in 2 days
items.detail: IRGC spokesman BG Ali Mohammad Naeini claimed 650 American casualties in first two days, including 160 at US Navy 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain. Pentagon and multiple independent sources disproved.

items.label: IRGC claim: 100+ Americans killed in Dubai
items.detail: IRGC claimed wave of attacks on US bases in Dubai killed 100+ American military and civilian personnel. US State Department called it 'complete disinformation' — no one killed or injured at US Consulate in Dubai.

items.label: Pentagon confirmed count: 13 US killed, 300+ wounded
items.detail: Actual Pentagon-confirmed US casualties: 13 killed (7 by enemy fire + 6 in KC-135 crash), 300+ wounded across 7 countries. 88% of wounded returned to duty. IRGC claims of hundreds killed are systematically fabricated.
